# SOC 2 CC7.1 Compliance Readiness Assessment
## Stratos Systems — Vulnerability Management Review

**Assessment Date:** December 28, 2025
**Auditor:** Compliance Readiness Assessment
**Control:** CC7.1 — Detection and Monitoring of Vulnerabilities
**Evidence Reviewed:** POL-VM-002 v4.0 | Nessus Q4 2025 Scan Report (33 findings)

---

## Methodology

Each open vulnerability in the scan report was cross-referenced against POL-VM-002 remediation SLAs using the following calculation:

> **Days Overdue** = Days elapsed from `first_detected` to assessment date (December 28, 2025) minus the applicable SLA window

Remediated items were reviewed for timeliness and documentation quality. Policy provisions were independently assessed for structural completeness.

---

## SLA Breach Analysis — Open Findings

The table below summarizes all open vulnerabilities with SLA status as of December 28, 2025.

| CVE | Host | Severity | CVSS | First Detected | SLA (days) | SLA Deadline | Days Since Detection | SLA Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CVE-2025-22224 | esxi-host-04 | **Critical** | 9.3 | 2025-09-01 | 15 | 2025-09-16 | 118 | **103 days overdue** |
| CVE-2024-53677 | legacy-portal | Medium | 5.8 | 2025-10-22 | 90 | 2026-01-20 | 67 | Within SLA |
| CVE-2025-21391 | web-proxy-01 | Medium | 5.5 | 2025-10-12 | 90 | 2026-01-10 | 77 | Within SLA |
| CVE-2025-21418 | corp-dc-02 | Medium | 6.2 | 2025-11-03 | 90 | 2026-02-01 | 55 | Within SLA |
| CVE-2024-11053 | build-agent-02 | Low | 3.4 | 2025-09-15 | Best effort | N/A | 104 | N/A (best effort) |
| CVE-2025-21376 | corp-dc-01 | Medium | 6.5 | 2025-11-10 | 90 | 2026-02-08 | 48 | Within SLA |
| CVE-2025-21298 | hr-workstation-14 | Medium | 6.8 | 2025-11-15 | 90 | 2026-02-13 | 43 | Within SLA |
| CVE-2025-21334 | hyperv-host-02 | Low | 3.8 | 2025-11-22 | Best effort | N/A | 36 | N/A (best effort) |
| CVE-2025-0411 | dev-ws-07 | Low | 3.2 | 2025-11-01 | Best effort | N/A | 57 | N/A (best effort) |
| CVE-2025-24085 | exec-macbook-03 | Medium | 5.9 | 2025-10-30 | 90 | 2026-01-28 | 59 | Within SLA |
| CVE-2025-21396 | exchange-srv-01 | Medium | 4.7 | 2025-12-01 | 90 | 2026-03-01 | 27 | Within SLA |
| CVE-2025-21293 | corp-dc-02 | Low | 2.8 | 2025-11-18 | Best effort | N/A | 40 | N/A (best effort) |
| CVE-2025-21355 | corp-ws-22 | Low | 2.5 | 2025-12-10 | Best effort | N/A | 18 | N/A (best effort) |
| CVE-2024-11680 | internal-share-01 | Medium | 5.2 | 2025-10-01 | 90 | 2025-12-30 | 88 | **2 days remaining** |
| CVE-2025-21311 | print-server-01 | Medium | 4.9 | 2025-10-25 | 90 | 2026-01-23 | 64 | Within SLA |
| CVE-2024-10914 | nas-backup-01 | Low | 3.6 | 2025-09-22 | Best effort | N/A | 97 | N/A (best effort) |
| CVE-2025-21295 | app-server-01 | Medium | 5.7 | 2025-11-12 | 90 | 2026-02-10 | 46 | Within SLA |
| CVE-2025-21297 | rdp-farm-01 | Medium | 6.0 | 2025-12-05 | 90 | 2026-03-05 | 23 | Within SLA |
| CVE-2025-21294 | legacy-app-srv | Medium | 6.7 | 2025-11-20 | 90 | 2026-02-18 | 38 | Within SLA |
| CVE-2025-0065 | remote-support-01 | Medium | 4.5 | 2025-12-08 | 90 | 2026-03-08 | 20 | Within SLA |

> **Note on CVE-2025-0282 (Ivanti Connect Secure, vpn-gateway-01):** This finding was classified and scanned as Medium (CVSS 5.4), but Ivanti CVE-2025-0282 carries a CVSS score of **9.0** in the National Vulnerability Database and was subject to active exploitation at time of disclosure. This classification warrants specific attention in Gap Finding #3 below.

---

## Gap Findings

---

### FINDING 01 — Critical SLA Breach: VMware ESXi Heap Overflow RCE Unpatched for 103 Days

**Severity:** 🔴 Critical

**Related Policy Section:** POL-VM-002 §4 (Vulnerability Classification), §4.1 (SLA Calculation), §5.1 (Patch Management), §6 (Risk Acceptance)

**CVE:** CVE-2025-22224 | **Host:** esxi-host-04 | **CVSS:** 9.3 | **First Detected:** September 1, 2025

**Finding Detail:**

This is the most significant compliance gap in the evidence set. CVE-2025-22224, a VMware ESXi heap overflow vulnerability enabling remote code execution, was first detected on September 1, 2025. Under POL-VM-002 §4, Critical vulnerabilities (CVSS ≥ 9.0) carry a 15-calendar-day remediation SLA, placing the deadline at **September 16, 2025**. As of the assessment date of December 28, 2025, this vulnerability remains **Open**, representing a breach of **103 days** beyond the policy-mandated deadline.

The remediation note references VULN-4471 and cites the need for host evacuation and coordination with VMware support. While technically complex remediations are acknowledged to present operational challenges, the policy provides a formal mechanism for such situations: the **Risk Acceptance process** defined in §6, which requires CISO approval, documented compensating controls, and an annual review cycle.

No evidence was provided indicating that a formal risk acceptance has been completed for this finding. The absence of risk acceptance documentation for a 103-day overdue critical RCE vulnerability on a hypervisor host is a significant control failure under CC7.1, which requires that detection and monitoring procedures support the entity's ability to *respond* to vulnerabilities — not merely identify them.

**Gap Summary:**
- Critical SLA deadline missed by 103 days with no evidence of formal risk acceptance
- No compensating controls documented in the scan record
- Hypervisor-level RCE exposure with no remediation date committed
- §5.1 requires critical patches via emergency change process — no evidence this was invoked

---

### FINDING 02 — Impending SLA Breach: ProjectSend Authentication Bypass Approaching Deadline

**Severity:** 🟡 Medium

**Related Policy Section:** POL-VM-002 §4 (Vulnerability Classification), §4.1 (SLA Calculation)

**CVE:** CVE-2024-11680 | **Host:** internal-share-01 | **CVSS:** 5.2 | **First Detected:** October 1, 2025

**Finding Detail:**

CVE-2024-11680, an authentication bypass in ProjectSend, was first detected on October 1, 2025. The applicable 90-day Medium SLA places the remediation deadline at **December 30, 2025** — two days from the assessment date. The finding remains **Open**, with the remediation note stating only that the tool is restricted to VPN access.

While VPN restriction may reduce exploitability, it does not constitute a documented compensating control under §6 of the policy, which requires formal risk acceptance with CISO approval to extend beyond SLA. The note in the scan report appears to be an informal justification entered by the asset owner rather than a formally approved risk acceptance record.

Unless remediation or a formal risk acceptance is completed by December 30, 2025, this finding will breach its SLA. This item should be escalated immediately to the asset owner and the Security Steering Committee.

**Gap Summary:**
- SLA expires in two days with no remediation in progress
- VPN restriction cited as informal justification but not formally documented as a compensating control per §6
- No risk acceptance record evidenced

---

### FINDING 03 — Potential Severity Misclassification: Ivanti CVE-2025-0282

**Severity:** 🟠 High

**Related Policy Section:** POL-VM-002 §4 (Vulnerability Classification), §3.1 (Scan Tools)

**CVE:** CVE-2025-0282 | **Host:** vpn-gateway-01 | **Scan CVSS:** 5.4 | **NVD CVSS:** 9.0 | **First Detected:** October 28, 2025 | **Status:** Remediated October 30

**Finding Detail:**

The Nessus scan report classifies CVE-2025-0282 (Ivanti Connect Secure Stack-Based Buffer Overflow) with a CVSS score of **5.4**, resulting in a Medium classification. However, the National Vulnerability Database assigns this vulnerability a CVSS v3.1 base score of **9.0**, which would place it in the **Critical** tier under POL-VM-002 §4.

This discrepancy is significant for two reasons:

1. **Retrospective Classification Accuracy:** While this finding was remediated on October 30 — two days after detection — the rapid response suggests the security team likely recognized the true severity. However, if the formal record reflects a Medium classification, SLA tracking, reporting metrics, and trend analysis will be understated.

2. **Process Integrity:** POL-VM-002 §4 bases classification on "CVSS v3.1 base scores" without specifying whether the organization uses the scanner-reported score or the authoritative NVD score. When these diverge — particularly for actively exploited vulnerabilities — the policy provides no guidance on adjudication. Scanners may use vendor-provided CVSS scores that differ from NVD scores, which can result in systematic underclassification of high-severity findings.

The fact that this vulnerability affects a VPN gateway (internet-facing, high-value target) and was subject to active exploitation at the time of disclosure amplifies the concern. The organization should be commended for the rapid two-day remediation, but the classification process gap warrants remediation.

**Gap Summary:**
- Scan report CVSS (5.4) materially differs from NVD CVSS (9.0) for an actively exploited vulnerability
- Policy §4 does not specify authoritative CVSS source when scanner and NVD scores conflict
- Misclassification risk may result in underreporting of Critical findings to the Security Steering Committee

---

### FINDING 04 — Missing Formal Risk Acceptance Documentation for Compensating Control Claims

**Severity:** 🟠 High

**Related Policy Section:** POL-VM-002 §6 (Risk Acceptance)

**Affected Findings:**
| CVE | Host | Notes Entry |
|---|---|---|
| CVE-2025-21311 | print-server-01 | "NTLMv1 disabled — compensating control in place" |
| CVE-2025-21294 | legacy-app-srv | "compensating control (network segmentation) documented" |
| CVE-2024-11680 | internal-share-01 | "access restricted to VPN" |
| CVE-2024-10914 | nas-backup-01 | "Isolated network segment" |

**Finding Detail:**

Four open findings in the scan report reference compensating controls or mitigating circumstances in the remediation notes field without any evidence that a formal risk acceptance has been completed per §6. The policy is explicit: vulnerabilities that cannot be remediated within SLA "may be accepted through the formal risk acceptance process requiring **CISO approval**, documented compensating controls, and **annual review**."

The distinction between an informal note entered by an asset owner and a formally approved risk acceptance is material to CC7.1. A scan report remediation notes field is not a substitute for a risk register entry, a risk acceptance form, or a documented CISO approval. For a SOC 2 Type II audit, auditors will expect to see the trail from vulnerability identification through to either verified remediation or a formally approved and documented risk acceptance record.

Notably, CVE-2025-21311 (Windows NTLM V1 Elevation of Privilege on print-server-01) represents the strongest compensating control claim, as NTLMv1 being disabled would logically mitigate the finding. However, without a formal closure or risk acceptance record, the finding remains open and undocumented from a governance perspective. The scan system and Jira project (VULN) should reflect a formal disposition.

**Gap Summary:**
- No evidence of formal risk acceptance records (CISO-approved) for any of the four findings claiming compensating controls
- Remediation notes field used as informal substitute for governance documentation
- §6 compliance cannot be demonstrated without formal risk acceptance artifacts for SLA-extended findings

---

### FINDING 05 — Open Vulnerability on Legacy System with No Remediation Date and SLA at Risk

**Severity:** 🟡 Medium

**Related Policy Section:** POL-VM-002 §4.1 (SLA Calculation), §5.1 (Patch Management), §6 (Risk Acceptance)

**CVE:** CVE-2024-53677 | **Host:** legacy-portal | **CVSS:** 5.8 | **First Detected:** October 22, 2025

**Finding Detail:**

CVE-2024-53677, an Apache Struts file upload path traversal vulnerability, was first detected on October 22, 2025 and remains open. The remediation note states the "Application being migrated to new stack — target March 2026." The 90-day Medium SLA deadline falls on **January 20, 2026** — 23 days from the assessment date.

The planned migration to a new stack in March 2026 will not meet the January 20 SLA deadline, and no interim compensating controls are documented. The remediation note implies the patch will not be applied because the application will eventually be decommissioned — a common and sometimes reasonable operational decision — but one that requires formal risk acceptance under §6 rather than a note in a scan report.

Furthermore, Apache Struts path traversal vulnerabilities have a documented history of exploitation (cf. Equifax breach, CVE-2017-5638). The presence of an unpatched file upload vulnerability on a legacy portal without documented network-level compensating controls warrants escalation regardless of the migration timeline.

**Gap Summary:**
- Planned remediation (March 2026 migration) will miss the January 20, 2026 SLA by approximately 70 days
- No compensating controls documented for the interim period
- No formal risk acceptance evidenced
- Legacy portal exposure not mitigated by any network restriction noted in the record

---

### FINDING 06 — Scan Coverage Gap: Development and Staging Assets Under-Represented

**Severity:** 🟡 Medium

**Related Policy Section:** POL-VM-002 §2 (Scope), §3.2 (Scan Frequency)

**Finding Detail:**

POL-VM-002 §2 explicitly includes "staging environments" and "development infrastructure" within scope, and §3.2 mandates quarterly scanning for development/staging assets. The Q4 2025 Nessus report contains 33 findings across production systems, corporate endpoints, and infrastructure — but only **two development-classified assets** appear in the scan data: `build-agent-02` (DevOps) and `dev-ws-07` (Development).

For a Q4 2025 quarterly scan to satisfy §3.2 for development and staging environments, the report should include results from all development and staging infrastructure. The near-absence of such findings likely reflects one of two scenarios: (a) development and staging systems were not scanned in Q4 2025, which constitutes a scan coverage gap; or (b) they were scanned and produced no findings, in which case those clean scan results should be included in or referenced alongside this report to demonstrate coverage.

The absence of evidence of staging coverage is particularly notable because §5.1 requires that "all patches are tested in staging before production deployment." If staging systems are not being scanned, the patch validation environment itself may carry undetected vulnerabilities, undermining the integrity of the patch testing process.

**Gap Summary:**
- Only 2 of 33 findings originate from development/staging assets despite policy-mandated quarterly coverage
- No evidence of clean scan results for development/staging to confirm coverage occurred
- Staging environment scanning gaps undermine the integrity of the patch testing process described in §5.1
- CC7.1 requires detection procedures to cover the full asset population

---

### FINDING 07 — Snyk and AWS Inspector Results Absent from Evidence Package

**Severity:** 🟡 Medium

**Related Policy Section:** POL-VM-002 §3.1 (Scan Tools)

**Finding Detail:**

POL-VM-002 §3.1 defines three scanning tools as part of the vulnerability management program: Nessus Professional (infrastructure), **Snyk** (open-source dependency scanning in CI/CD pipelines), and **AWS Inspector** (cloud workload vulnerability assessment). The evidence package provided for this assessment includes only the Nessus scan report.

No Snyk scan results and no AWS Inspector findings have been provided. This creates a material gap in the evidence base for CC7.1, which requires the entity to demonstrate detection and monitoring procedures across its asset population. Given that POL-VM-002 §2 includes "cloud resources (AWS, Azure)" in scope, and that the Nessus report does not appear to include cloud-native workloads, the AWS Inspector gap may result in cloud vulnerabilities being entirely undetected or unreviewed within this assessment cycle.

For a SOC 2 Type II examination, auditors will expect population-level evidence across all tools named in the policy. The absence of Snyk and AWS Inspector outputs either indicates these tools are not operating as described in the policy (a control design gap) or that evidence collection was incomplete (a readiness gap that must be resolved before audit).

**Gap Summary:**
- Snyk dependency scan results not provided — CI/CD pipeline vulnerabilities unverifiable
- AWS Inspector results not provided — cloud workload coverage unverifiable
- Policy names three tools; only one tool's evidence submitted
- Azure cloud resources mentioned in §2 scope but no corresponding scan tool defined in §3.1

> **Bonus observation on Azure:** The policy scope in §2 includes Azure as a cloud platform, but §3.1 lists only AWS Inspector for cloud vulnerability assessment. No Azure-equivalent scanning tool (e.g., Microsoft Defender for Cloud) is defined. This represents a policy coverage gap for Azure workloads independent of the evidence submission issue.

---

### FINDING 08 — "Best Effort" SLA Tier Lacks Measurable Compliance Criteria

**Severity:** 🟡 Medium

**Related Policy Section:** POL-VM-002 §4 (Vulnerability Classification)

**Finding Detail:**

POL-VM-002 §4 defines remediation SLAs for Critical, High, Medium, and Low severity vulnerabilities. Low severity vulnerabilities (CVSS 0.1–3.9) are assigned a remediation target of "**Best effort**" with no defined time boundary.

While it is defensible to assign lower urgency to Low severity findings, the complete absence of a measurable SLA for this tier creates a compliance monitoring gap. Five open Low severity findings appear in the Q4 2025 scan report, some with significant aging:

| CVE | Host | First Detected | Days Open |
|---|---|---|---|
| CVE-2024-11053 | build-agent-02 | 2025-09-15 | 104 |
| CVE-2024-10914 | nas-backup-01 | 2025-09-22 | 97 |
| CVE-2025-21334 | hyperv-host-02 | 2025-11-22 | 36 |
| CVE-2025-0411 | dev-ws-07 | 2025-11-01 | 57 |
| CVE-2025-21293 | corp-dc-02 | 2025-11-18 | 40 |

CVE-2024-11053 (curl HSTS Bypass on build-agent-02) has been open for **104 days**, and CVE-2024-10914 (D-Link NAS Command Injection) has been open for **97 days**. Without a defined outer boundary for Low findings, there is no mechanism to trigger review, escalation, or formal risk acceptance for aging Low vulnerabilities — some of which (particularly a command injection on a NAS backup server) may warrant reconsideration of their severity classification.

For SOC 2 purposes, "best effort" is not auditable. A control that cannot be measured cannot be demonstrated as operating effectively. Industry practice typically establishes a 180-day or one-year outer boundary for Low severity findings before mandatory review.

**Gap Summary:**
- "Best effort" provides no measurable SLA for auditors to assess compliance
- Low findings aging to 97–104 days with no escalation trigger
- D-Link NAS Command Injection (CVE-2024-10914) on a backup server may warrant severity reconsideration regardless of network isolation
- No policy mechanism to force review of perpetually open Low findings

---

### FINDING 09 — Internet-Facing Assets Receiving Only Weekly Scans Despite Critical Exposure Profile

**Severity:** 🟡 Medium

**Related Policy Section:** POL-VM-002 §3.2 (Scan Frequency)

**Finding Detail:**

POL-VM-002 §3.2 defines weekly scanning for internet-facing systems. While weekly scanning exceeds the frequency for internal assets, this cadence may be insufficient given the current threat environment and the nature of assets identified in the scan data.

The scan report includes internet-facing or perimeter assets such as:
- `fw-external-01` (external firewall, Palo Alto)
- `vpn-gateway-01` (Ivanti VPN gateway)
- `esxi-host-04` (ESXi host accessible on port 443)
- `fortimanager-01` (network management platform)

CVE-2025-0282 on vpn-gateway-01 is a notable example: this vulnerability was first detected on October 28, 2025 and patched on October 30, demonstrating that when high-severity findings are identified, the team responds rapidly. However, if a critical vulnerability is introduced between weekly scan cycles, the organization may have a 7-day detection window for internet-facing assets.

NIST SP 800-40r4 and emerging SOC 2 audit guidance increasingly expect continuous monitoring or near-real-time detection for high-value internet-exposed assets, particularly in environments processing sensitive data. The policy does not define a continuous monitoring approach (e.g., AWS Inspector continuous scanning mode, real-time agent-based detection) as a complement to scheduled scans.

**Gap Summary:**
- Weekly scheduled scanning creates up to a 7-day detection window for new vulnerabilities on internet-facing assets
- No continuous or agent-based monitoring defined for high-value perimeter systems
- Policy does not address newly published CVEs between scan cycles for internet-facing hosts
- CC7.1 specifically references detection of "newly discovered vulnerabilities" — weekly scan cadence may not meet this intent for perimeter assets

---

### FINDING 10 — Scan Credential Rotation and Authenticated Scan Validation Not Evidenced

**Severity:** 🟡 Medium

**Related Policy Section:** POL-VM-002 §3.3 (Authenticated Scanning)

**Finding Detail:**

POL-VM-002 §3.3 requires that all infrastructure scans use authenticated (credentialed) scanning and that scan credentials stored in CyberArk are rotated quarterly. The Nessus scan report provides finding-level output but does not include scan configuration metadata, scan policy details, or credential validation confirmation that would allow an auditor to verify that scans were in fact conducted with authenticated credentials.

Unauthenticated scans significantly reduce detection coverage — particularly for local privilege escalation vulnerabilities, missing patches, and misconfigurations that require agent-level access to enumerate. If scans are running without valid credentials (e.g., due to a failed rotation or access issue), the scan report will appear complete but will materially undercount actual vulnerabilities present.

For CC7.1 compliance, the organization should be able to provide: (a) evidence of quarterly credential rotation in CyberArk, (b) scan configuration exports showing authenticated scan policies are applied, and (c) scan completion reports that indicate the percentage of hosts successfully scanned with authentication versus unauthenticated fallback.

**Gap Summary:**
- No evidence provided that scans were conducted with authenticated credentials as required by §3.3
- No credential rotation logs or CyberArk confirmation included in evidence package
- Cannot verify detection coverage accuracy without authenticated scan confirmation
- Unauthenticated scans would systematically undercount vulnerabilities, creating a false picture of security posture

---

## Summary of Findings

| # | Finding Title | Severity | Policy Section | SLA Breach |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 01 | Critical VMware ESXi RCE Unpatched 103 Days | 🔴 Critical | §4, §4.1, §5.1, §6 | Yes — 103 days overdue |
| 02 | ProjectSend Auth Bypass Approaching SLA Expiry | 🟡 Medium | §4, §4.1 | Imminent — expires Dec 30 |
| 03 | Ivanti CVE-2025-0282 Potential Severity Misclassification | 🟠 High | §4, §3.1 | N/A (remediated) |
| 04 | Missing Formal Risk Acceptance for Compensating Controls | 🟠 High | §6 | Governance gap |
| 05 | Legacy Portal Apache Struts Unpatched — Migration Won't Meet SLA | 🟡 Medium | §4.1, §5.1, §6 | Will breach Jan 20, 2026 |
| 06 | Development/Staging Scan Coverage Gap | 🟡 Medium | §2, §3.2 | Coverage gap |
| 07 | Snyk and AWS Inspector Evidence Not Provided | 🟡 Medium | §3.1 | Evidence gap |
| 08 | Low Severity "Best Effort" SLA Is Not Auditable | 🟡 Medium | §4 | Policy gap |
| 09 | Weekly Scan Cadence Insufficient for Internet-Facing Assets | 🟡 Medium | §3.2 | Design gap |
| 10 | Authenticated Scan Compliance Not Evidenced | 🟡 Medium | §3.3 | Evidence gap |

---

## Recommended Remediation Actions

### Immediate (Before December 31, 2025)
1. **Initiate formal risk acceptance for esxi-host-04 (CVE-2025-22224)** — obtain CISO approval, document compensating controls (e.g., network segmentation of ESXi management interface, IDS/IPS rules for port 443), and establish a firm remediation date. This is the highest priority action.
2. **Remediate or formally risk-accept CVE-2024-11680 on internal-share-01** before the December 30 SLA deadline.

### Short-Term (January 2026)
3. **Establish formal risk acceptance records** for all four findings claiming compensating controls (print-server-01, legacy-app-srv, internal-share-01, nas-backup-01). File in the VULN Jira project with CISO approval artifacts.
4. **Resolve CVSS scoring source ambiguity** in POL-VM-002 §4 — specify whether NVD or scanner-reported scores govern classification, with NVD as the authoritative source when discrepancies exist.
5. **Initiate formal risk acceptance for legacy-portal (CVE-2024-53677)** with documented compensating controls bridging to the March 2026 migration.
6. **Provide Snyk and AWS Inspector evidence** for the assessment period; if tools are not operational as described in policy, update §3.1 to reflect current state.
7. **Address Azure scanning gap** in §3.1 by defining a tool (e.g., Microsoft Defender for Cloud) for Azure workload coverage.

### Policy Updates (Q1 2026)
8. **Define a measurable outer bound for Low severity findings** (e.g., 180-day mandatory review trigger) in §4.
9. **Define a continuous monitoring strategy** for internet-facing assets in §3.2 to supplement scheduled weekly scans.
10. **Add evidence requirements to §3.3** specifying that authenticated scan confirmation and credential rotation logs must be retained as audit evidence each quarter.

---

## Overall CC7.1 Readiness Assessment

| Dimension | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Vulnerability Detection (Scanning Coverage) | ⚠️ Partial | Nessus coverage evidenced; Snyk/AWS Inspector/Azure absent |
| Scan Frequency Compliance | ⚠️ Partial | Dev/staging coverage not demonstrated; internet-facing cadence may be insufficient |
| Severity Classification | ⚠️ Partial | CVSS source ambiguity identified; potential misclassification on CVE-2025-0282 |
| SLA Compliance — Critical | ❌ Non-Compliant | One critical finding 103 days overdue with no risk acceptance |
| SLA Compliance — High | ✅ N/A | No open High findings in current scan data |
| SLA Compliance — Medium | ⚠️ At Risk | One finding expiring in 2 days; one will breach in January |
| Risk Acceptance Process | ❌ Non-Compliant | Compensating controls claimed informally without §6 governance artifacts |
| Remediation Tracking | ✅ Adequate | Jira tracking and monthly reporting to Steering Committee evidenced |
| Authenticated Scanning | ⚠️ Unverified | Policy requires it; evidence of compliance not provided |

> **Readiness Conclusion:** Stratos Systems has established a sound policy framework for vulnerability management and demonstrates active remediation activity across the majority of findings. However, the unresolved Critical SLA breach for esxi-host-04, the absence of formal risk acceptance documentation, and gaps in tool evidence coverage represent material findings that would likely result in a qualified opinion or exceptions under a SOC 2 Type II examination if not remediated prior to audit fieldwork.

---

*This assessment is based solely on the evidence provided. Findings are limited to what can be determined from the policy document and Nessus scan report. A complete CC7.1 assessment would additionally require review of Jira VULN project records, Snyk and AWS Inspector outputs, CyberArk credential rotation logs, risk acceptance register, and Security Steering Committee meeting minutes.*
